description: Analyze San Francisco building data in the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/historic_analysis.ipynb using the HDF5 data store at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Join the 'buildings' and 'parcels' tables to associate buildings with 'zone_id'. Filter for residential properties where residential_sales_price > 0 and year_built > 1800, then categorize them as 'Historic' (year_built < 1940) or 'Modern' (year_built >= 1940). Compute zone-level metrics including historic_count, modern_count, total_buildings, pct_historic, avg_historic_price, and avg_modern_price. Filter the results for zones with at least 50 total buildings and 10 historic buildings, sorted by avg_historic_price descending. Export the final table to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/historic_zones.csv and save a scatter plot of pct_historic vs. avg_historic_price to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/historic_scatter.png. Ensure the notebook is fully executed.
